summary: The core gap is that local PGlite databases NEVER get auto-migrated at startup — only remote PG does. A prerequisite blocker: the PGlite migration path in db.pg.ts is NOT idempotent (bare CREATE TABLE, no tracking table), so adding auto-migration would break on 2nd startup. Strategy: make PGlite path idempotent first, then wire auto-migration into the local adapter. Clean up 7 pieces of SQLite dead code afterward. Document the completed philosophy.

root_causes.hypothesis: Local PGlite migration gap: makeLocalPgAdapter() never calls applyMigrations()
evidence: adapter.ts:86-153: makeLocalPgAdapter() creates adapter wrapper but has zero migration calls
adapter.ts:155-158: LocalPgAdapter layer is Effect.sync(() => makeLocalPgAdapter()) — synchronous, no Effect.gen for async migration
adapter.ts:194: makePgAdapter() has the pattern we need: `applyMigrations(client).catch(...)` — fire-and-forget
db.ts:64-76: Client() singleton factory also never calls applyMigrations()
Gap report confirms: 'makeLocalPgAdapter() (adapter.ts:86-153) does NOT call applyMigrations()'

root_causes.hypothesis: PGlite migration path is NOT idempotent — blocks safe auto-migration
evidence: db.pg.ts:77-84: PGlite path reads ALL migration files and executes ALL SQL every call — no skip logic
db.pg.ts:73: Uses `readMigrationFiles()` which reads from disk, not a tracking table
No `__drizzle_migrations` table created or queried in PGlite path
Migration SQL uses bare `CREATE TABLE` (not `IF NOT EXISTS`) — Drizzle generates non-idempotent DDL
Migration #4 (blushing_zuras) uses ALTER TABLE — would fail on re-run if column already jsonb
Test file migration.pg.test.ts only tests single applyMigrations call — never tests idempotency

root_causes.hypothesis: skipMigrations runtime flag is defined but never wired to gate migration application
evidence: db.ts:64-76: `Client()` factory reads flags including skipMigrations but ignores it completely
adapter.ts: Neither makeLocalPgAdapter nor makePgAdapter check skipMigrations
Users cannot opt-out of auto-migration even though the env var exists

fixes.title: Make PGlite migration path idempotent
fixes.description: Replicate Drizzle's migration-tracking pattern in the PGlite path of applyMigrations(). Create __drizzle_migrations table on first run, skip already-applied migrations on subsequent runs. This is the PREREQUISITE for fix-B.
risks: PGlite .query() API may differ from node-postgres pool.query() — verify result shape {rows: [...]}
SQL injection via migration.hash in INSERT — use parameterized query instead of string interpolation
Drizzle's built-in migration.hash format may differ from what readMigrationFiles() returns — verify field name
Tracked migrations table adds a DDL statement to startup — first-run cold start will be slightly slower
Transaction wrapping: PGlite must support BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK exec() calls. PGlite uses PostgreSQL-compatible SQL — this is standard.
Parameterized INSERT: migration.hash is a SHA-256 hex string (Drizzle-generated). SQL injection risk is minimal but escaping via template literal is used defensively.
validation: 1. bun test packages/opencode/test/storage/migration.pg.test.ts — existing tests must still pass
2. Add test: applyMigrations twice on same :memory: DB — 2nd call must succeed with no error
3. Add test: __drizzle_migrations table exists after applyMigrations
4. Add test: migration hash recorded after application
5. bun typecheck in packages/opencode
6. Verify partial-failure safety: insert deliberate syntax error mid-migration, confirm BEGIN/ROLLBACK undoes prior DDL in that file

fixes.title: Auto-apply migrations for local PGlite at startup
fixes.description: Mirror the fire-and-forget pattern from makePgAdapter() into makeLocalPgAdapter(). Add `applyMigrations(client).catch(...)` after adapter construction, gated behind skipMigrations flag.

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/storage/adapter-dual-write.ts (imports makeLocalPgAdapter — needs update if we change signature)
impact.type_changes: makeLocalPgAdapter gains optional `options?: { skipMigrations?: boolean }` parameter

risks: DELAY: makeLocalPgAdapter is synchronous (Effect.sync), but applyMigrations is async. The fire-and-forget pattern handles this (void promise, errors logged). However, the first query to the DB might arrive before migrations complete. Mitigation: the existing makePgAdapter pattern also has this race; it's accepted.
PGlite client accessor: `(Database.Client() as any).$client` may return undefined if Client() hasn't been called yet. Need to verify call order in adapter.ts LocalPgAdapter layer.
adapter-dual-write.ts imports makeLocalPgAdapter — if we change the signature, dual-write must also be updated (though it's dead code, so build breaks are possible).

skipMigrations: The flag comes from RuntimeFlags (Effect layer), which may not be available during synchronous makeLocalPgAdapter. The Client() in db.ts accesses flags via Effect.runSync — same pattern works here.
validation: 1. Start app with local PGlite (no OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL) — verify tables exist after startup
2. Start app a second time — verify idempotency: no crash, no duplicate tables
3. Set OPENCODE_SKIP_MIGRATIONS=true — verify migrations are NOT auto-applied
4. Set OPENCODE_SKIP_MIGRATIONS=false (or unset) — verify migrations ARE auto-applied
5. bun typecheck in packages/opencode
6. All existing tests still pass

fixes.title: Wire skipMigrations flag through makePgAdapter
fixes.description: makePgAdapter already calls applyMigrations but doesn't honor the skipMigrations flag. Add the flag check.
risks: readRuntimeFlags may not be exported from db.ts — need to verify or use Database.getPath-like pattern
Minimal risk — same pattern already works in db.ts Client() factory
validation: 1. OPENCODE_SKIP_MIGRATIONS=true with remote PG → migrations skipped
2. bun typecheck

fixes.title: Fix build.ts dead migration-loading code
fixes.description: The build.ts references a deleted `migration/` directory. OPENCODE_MIGRATIONS env var is set but NEVER consumed in src/. Two options: (A) remove the dead block entirely, or (B) update to migration-pg/.

tests_to_run: any tests that inspect binary env vars

risks: If OPENCODE_MIGRATIONS is consumed at runtime by native/binary code we haven't found — but grep for OPENCODE_MIGRATIONS in packages/opencode/src/ returns zero results
If CI uses the env var in packed binaries — the env var will now be absent. Since nothing reads it, this is safe.
Build script might be using migration data for something else in lines 30-55 — need to read full context before deletion
validation: 1. Search for OPENCODE_MIGRATIONS in entire repo — confirm zero consumers in runtime code
2. bun run build from packages/opencode — must not error on missing migration/ dir
3. Run any smoke tests that use the built binary

fixes.title: Delete or decommission adapter-dual-write.ts
fixes.description: adapter-dual-write.ts is NEVER imported (confirmed: zero imports across entire codebase). It wraps makeLocalPgAdapter + makePgAdapter in a dual-write pattern using SQLite terminology. Dead code.
fixes.after: DELETED FILE — record finding if deletion uncertain, or just delete
risks: Zero imports → zero consumers → zero risk of deletion
Low risk of it being referenced by external config or dynamic import path
Mild risk: future integration with PG shadow persistence may need this pattern — keep finding as reference
validation: 1. Confirm zero imports with: rg 'adapter-dual-write' packages/opencode/src/ packages/opencode/test/
2. bun typecheck
3. Full test suite

fixes.title: Rename SQLiteTransactionOptions → LocalTransactionOptions
fixes.description: The type SQLiteTransactionOptions (adapter.ts:53) has SQLite naming but is used with PGlite (Postgres dialect). Type tag is '_tag: sqlite'. Rename and update tag.
risks: Internal update only — these types are not exported from any index barrel
The _tag: 'sqlite' check at adapter.ts:130 inside makeLocalPgAdapter must be updated to _tag: 'local'
TransactionOptions union type at line 65 must also be updated
validation: 1. bun typecheck
2. All tests pass
3. Search for "_tag.*sqlite" in codebase — only adapter.ts should have references

fixes.title: Update canonical PG migration terrain map
fixes.description: Update the existing canonical terrain map at `docs/json/cartography/pg_migration_terrain.v1.json` with: (1) idempotency guarantees after Fix-A (__drizzle_migrations tracking, transaction wrapping), (2) auto-apply behavior for local PGlite after Fix-B, (3) OPENCODE_SKIP_MIGRATIONS flag usage, (4) how to add new migrations (bun run db:generate:pg), (5) data migration vs DDL migration distinction.
fixes.before: Existing terrain map describes eager but non-fatal philosophy, Drizzle Kit generation, and PG-only dialect — but lacks idempotency details and local PGlite auto-apply documentation.
fixes.after: Updated terrain map with new sections: 'Idempotency Guarantees' (__drizzle_migrations tracking + transaction wrapping), 'Local PGlite Auto-Migration', 'OPENCODE_SKIP_MIGRATIONS Flag', 'Adding Migrations' (drizzle-kit generate workflow). Validation: bun run db:generate:pg → drizzle-kit check → commit migration-pg/.
validation: 1. Validate against rig.documentation.page.v1 schema
2. Render via render_static_docs.py

rollback_plan: Each fix is independently reversible. Revert in any order. Fix-A (idempotency) and Fix-B (auto-migration) are linked — revert Fix-B first if needed, then Fix-A.

claim_adversary_findings.evidence: Called at adapter.ts:194 (makePgAdapter for remote PG) and migrate.ts:20 (CLI). NOT called for local PGlite.

claim_adversary_findings.claim: Migrations can be applied safely at startup
claim_adversary_findings.evidence: Only safe AFTER Fix-A (idempotency). Before Fix-A, PGlite path re-runs bare CREATE TABLE on every call — 2nd startup would crash.

Zero SQLite runtime code remains, but 7 pieces of SQLite-named code and dead code persist: SQLiteTransactionOptions, adapter-dual-write.ts (referencing SQLite), _SQLiteSatisfiesDLC, build.ts migration/ reference, db.ts opencode.db filename, skipMigrations dead flag, OPENCODE_MIGRATIONS dead env var.
Flag is defined, tested, and read at runtime, but NEVER wired to any migration code path. Setting OPENCODE_SKIP_MIGRATIONS=true has zero effect.

convention_notes.migration_naming: Drizzle default: YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_snake_case_name. This is established and should continue.
convention_notes.file_placement: migration-pg/ at packages/opencode level, not src/. Consistent with Drizzle convention.
convention_notes.auto_apply_pattern: Fire-and-forget .catch() — established in makePgAdapter. Errors logged, not propagated. Mirror this exactly in local path.
convention_notes.Layer_architecture: LocalPgAdapter is Effect.sync (synchronous). Fire-and-forget async migration is acceptable because the callback pattern is already used in makePgAdapter.
convention_notes.testing: Bun test with :memory: PGlite. No test framework needed beyond bun:test.

blindingly_obvious_but_right: The idempotency fix (A) is the minimum viable change to make auto-migration safe. Without it, adding auto-migration to local PGlite would be a regression.

The gap report says 'MEDIUM severity' for the local PGlite gap, but the idempotency blocker makes it closer to HIGH — users could corrupt their DB by manually running migrate.ts twice on the same PGlite file.
Seven pieces of SQLite dead code is a smell — but none are bugs. They are naming-only remnants from the migration. Cleanup is good hygiene but not urgent.

additional_risks_from_building_inspector.finding: Migration failure silently absorbed — app proceeds with broken schema
additional_risks_from_building_inspector.resolution: BY DESIGN: 'eager but non-fatal' is the established philosophy. If migrations fail, the DBA is expected to investigate logs. This is production-accepted — the app must not crash on migration failure.

additional_risks_from_building_inspector.finding: CLI db:migrate + app startup can race on same DB
additional_risks_from_building_inspector.resolution: Only relevant for PGlite file-backed mode (single process). For node-postgres, Drizzle's migrate() acquires advisory lock. For PGlite, the __drizzle_migrations tracking table prevents re-execution (added in Fix-A).

After Fix-B, local PGlite will auto-apply migrations. Tests using pgTestLayer must account for tables already existing. Current tests work because the fixture creates tables manually — after migration auto-apply, this may cause CREATE TABLE conflicts. Mitigation: update test fixture to use applyMigrations() or accept existing tables.

additional_risks_from_building_inspector.finding: No meta/_journal.json — ordering depends on directory name sort
additional_risks_from_building_inspector.resolution: Drizzle Kit v8+ uses per-directory snapshot.json with prevIds chain. readMigrationFiles() sorts directories lexicographically by name — safe. No journal file needed for PGlite path.
